title: sagina decumbens subsp. decumbens
intro: Sagina decumbens subsp. decumbens, also known as low sagina or wild perlagon, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This herb is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sagina decumbens subsp. decumbens include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant may be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
